title: Apple Reports First Quarter Results: Revenue $124.3 Billion, EPS $2.40
content: CUPERTINO, California — Apple today announced financial results for its fiscal 2026 first quarter ended December 28, 2025. Revenue reached $124.3 billion, up 4% year-over-year. iPhone revenue was $69.1 billion (+2% YoY), Mac revenue $9.0 billion (+16%), iPad $8.1 billion (+15%), Wearables and Home Accessories $11.7 billion (-2%), and Services a record $26.3 billion (+14%). International revenue accounted for 58% of total. Net income was $36.3 billion, or $2.40 per diluted share, compared to $33.9 billion in the year-ago quarter. Tim Cook, Apple's CEO, stated: 'We are thrilled to report a record Services quarter and continued strong momentum for Apple Intelligence, which is now available on all iPhone 16 models and recent Mac and iPad hardware. We continue to invest heavily in research and development to bring even more AI capabilities to our platforms.' CFO Kevan Parekh noted that gross margin was 46.9%, up from 45.9% in Q1 FY2025, reflecting the higher-margin Services mix. Apple ended the quarter with $65.2 billion in net cash and generated $30.1 billion in operating cash flow. The board declared a cash dividend of $0.25 per share, payable February 13, 2026.
